user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Tailspin Toys. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 11:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 15:13 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:06:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:13:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:21:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
